one_liner: Asana's Work Graph API offers full REST CRUD access with self-serve Personal Access Tokens (free tier included) or OAuth2 for multi-user apps, plus an official first-party MCP server.

auth_methods.notes: Personal Access Token (PAT) - long-lived bearer token, generated via developer console, quickest way to authenticate
Authorization code grant flow for multi-user apps; endpoints at app.asana.com/-/oauth_authorize and oauth_token
Service Accounts - Enterprise-tier only, org-wide programmatic access, created by super admins

self_serve_notes: Free Personal tier can create a PAT immediately via developer console and access the REST API, subject to lower rate limits (150 rpm vs 1500 rpm on paid tiers). Developer sandbox available for testing premium features.

api_surface.notes: Comprehensive RESTful API with predictable resource-oriented URLs, JSON responses, full CRUD on tasks/projects/workspaces/users etc. OpenAPI spec (oas.yaml) available, plus client libraries and API explorer.

evidence.supports: Details PAT, OAuth2, and Service Account authentication methods
Describes REST API structure, base URL, and PAT-based quick start
Self-serve PAT generation via developer console
OAuth2 flow details for multi-user apps

flags: Official first-party MCP server exists, reducing need for custom build but still buildable via REST API directly
Service Accounts restricted to Enterprise tier
